TESTO_ORIGINALE: RL=4.3 VV=4.5 🇮🇹🇮🇹🇮🇹 80%Corvina+15% Rondinella+5% Osleta Alegrini's family is the leader of the Valpolicella Classic Style. The reasons are: more than 520 years dedicated to the vineyard development in it's more than 100 hectares and its different drying processes.  👁Dark Ruby w a tawny rim 👃Fig, leather & Cocoa  💋Ripe Plum, sweet raisins, nutmeg, cinnamon, clove, Dark chocolate w big grains & sweet tannins  👚Full & Dense  💫 Marked by it's big grains tannins, dried fruit. ⏰Raisins longaftertaste
Gorgeous wine that ages very well. Dark garnet/ruby in colour. Smoke, leather, prune, mocha, tobacco, cherry, raisin and fig. Great structure with medium tannins.
To sum this up is a word, DELIZIOSO! Dense fruit (raisin) with an amazing, sophisticated, and very long finish. Wonderful aroma to start!!  Terrific.  2025, ACC.
Big and bold. 15.5% abv but settled down after being left and hour. And that lovely slightly sweet raisins nose with fig and earthy leather came out. Cracking bottle 🤙🍷
Very balanced easy to drink. Bit more bold, light bitter aftertaste from start. 30-40mins on air should be enough.   plum cherry tobacco chocolate raisin blackberry black cherry
Tasted at the CAL-ITAL Summer WineFest at the Golden Nugget Casino in Atlantic City, on Aug 29, 2015. A great wine festival presenting just a small pinch of California & Italian wines.
cherry plum tobacco oak leather blackberry vanilla prune black cherry dark fruit pepper Two words, shared brilliance minerals anise exotic spice graphite thyme rosemary dried herbs stone
It is dark purple color and concentrated  grape scent, a little  bit sweet. It is like dark cacao grape juice. I really enjoy drinking amarone with my sister. Pairing with lamb. 2016

A: Medium garnet N: pronounced aromas of rich strawberry, cherry, dried raisin, cedar, clove, dried herbs, earthy with leather. Developing.  P: Dry, high acidity, and full body with high alcohol and tannin. Dried fruit, blackberry and leather dominate as pronounced intense palate carries to long finish.   Drink or keep.   BYL4I4C3

Appearance is deep ruby,  Nose has pronounced intensity, prune, dried cherry, raisin, violet, molasses, nutmeg, cinnamon, clove, vanilla, pepper.  Palate is dry, with high acidity, high soft tannins, high alcohol, full body, pronounced flavour intensity, chocolate, pepper, liquorice. Long finish. Outstanding. Can drink now but can age.

It is very full bodied for a red wine, and medium bodied for an Amarone. It is medium textured with dark sparkly ruby eyes. It has a firm attack with hints of cherry, fig , and mocha. The finish is long and complex, however it is not as rich as most Amarones. It is fruit forward and the hints of oak turn into a raspberry, caramel cheesecake. It is also rare for the acidity to stand in front of the sugars, but with this Amarone the tart palate is first.

In Alberobello at restorante I’l Poeta Contandino with chef Leonardo. Dark garnet red, bouquet of cherry and plum, flavors of dark fruits, cherry, cocoa, hazelnuts, and oaky lush tannins. Modern method Appasamiento wine at its very best. One must graduate to these marvelous wines!!!

Aroma of figs, port, and dark fruit. Just a tine of tawny color on the rim. Rich and spicy, this is a complex. Opens with deep raisin flavor, chocolate, smoke, and finishes dry. Mouthfeel is full. Went superb with traditional bolognese and homemade fettucine. I would drink this again. And again.
2016: powerful and wink, brings all the corvina force into the glass with a touch of elegance and sweetness that helps to lower the alcohol tones… still really bold
Dark berries. Deep tannin. Dark chocolate. Fantastic wine.
4.3/5⭐️  chocolate, plum and raisin
cherry chocolate raisin plum tobacco oak leather blackberry
Delicious and smooth. Fruit forward and strong hints if chocolate and caramel
So smooth. Full body. Long finish. Wonderful.
Amazing amarone. Huge dark fruit and spice notes with a slight herbal menthol tone
Fantastic amarone - big, rich & dark.
Exceptional Amarone. Dry and spicy. Smoke, raisin, black currant.
Raisins, dates, cranberries. Smoke, tobacco, leather, cocoa, coffee
Like a rock women chocolate cherry plum tobacco oak leather
Smoother then most Italian reds. Just the right amount of earth mixed with black cherry.
Sweet, red cherry, lushes, velvet chocolate, tannins high lovely
One of the quality Amarone, this amarone is very rich flavor such as cherry, plum and chocolate. This was enough wine without any food.
Great wine. Had this at a blind tasting. raisin chocolate black cherry
Perfect acidity, so smooth
Blueberry jam on nose. Thin mouth feel, with a subtle acidity that builds with the finish.
Simply amazing, this Amarone knows how to hit the complexity and powerful flavors
Incredible nose of raisins, balsamic scents, new leather, fresh mud, herbs, toasted vanilla...in palate a well balanced mix of dark fruits, raisins, worn oak..a delightful essence.

Classic Amarone blend with Corvina, Corvinone, Rondinella and Oseleta. Vineyards with an average of 30 years. Ageing in new barriques for 18 months. Residual sugar of 4,5 g/l. Intense nose with aromas of matured-dried fruits and spices. Powerful but also some freshness to balance this quite too modern Amarone. 15/20.
Medium to deep red, complex and long finish with hint of sweetness. cherry plum raisin oak

Super smooth amarone. Nice, well-balanced tannins. Long finish. Black cherry, tobacco, some spice. Dark garnet color.  We had it with Greek food - balanced well with the lemon and acidity of the food. This is so smooth you could drink it with just about any food. Would also be great just by itself. Definitely saved to my favorites.

Allegrini Dinner Ristorante Armani.  Magnificent dinner in Ristorante Armani in April 2018 with Ms. Marilisa Allegrini to taste the allegrini wines, this was the star of the night, ruby in color with black fruit nose, smooth tannins with oak and spice on the palate. Memorable wine.
A serious wine. Blackcurrant, raisins, a gamey, mushroom aroma, lots of pepper, vanilla and cedar. A Big Wine.
Very deep colour, medium to full body. Fresh, piercing aromas of black cherry, and sweet spices on the nose.  Smooth and elegant tannins. One of the best wines I have tasted. 🍷 95/100
Still continues to be one of my favorite Amarone. Beautifully bold with a nice note of raisin and prune, paired with an oak and some chocolate. It pairs very well with steak dinner.
Drank with Mat and Aaron in Trattoria after meeting Caesars.    Highly rated but for me is thin and dies on the palate.   P.S Mat hated it.
This was a tanic wine that tasted of cherries. I would not have recognized it as an amarone because I didn't get any dried fruit on it.
Brilliant wine. Full bodied. Relatively smooth. Deep red in hue. Raspberry, plum, blackberry, chocolate, oak. Definitely worth a try.
One of the best Amarone at the price. Complex with a lot of depth. Strong blackberry on the nose with some earth. Beautiful and bold on palate.
lovely. this is the wine I had by the glass at an Italian restaurant near tate modern. frustratingly im not sure if it was this wine exactly.
Typical Amarone. Full body, high alcohol, acidity, pronounced intensity. Cherry, jam, forest floor, earth, chocolate. Long finish.
oak chocolate raisin blackberry prune smoke dried fruit blackcurrant
LA Puglia Las Angelas Woge. Practically perfect.

8/17/25 Vicino with Rob. This wine was perfectly paired with risotto. tobacco leather spicy with a dry finish.   Tasting Notes The controlled drying (appassimento) of the harvested grapes, which lasts about one hundred days, brings great aromatic intensity of dark fruit and undergrowth, together with spicy and tobacco notes. The impact on the palate is powerful and vigorous, with the wine gradually tapering on the acidic and savory momentum, extending gently with a clean, dry finish.
Wonderful amarone. It is so elegant, fruity and full body.

Dark purple in color with a barnyard, blueberry, and vanilla nose. Lots of wild red fruit in the taste, with chocolate, cola, cherry, spice, and earthy notes. “Wines We Ljke September 2024” wine tasting.

Amazing valpolicella! Deep ruby colour and tawnier towards the edge, one can find lots of preserved black fruits, raisins, morello cherries and chocolate on the nose. On the palate it is medium to full bodied with a complex smoky character. Well integrated alcohol and acidity levels.

2017. Medium ruby. A seductive smoky sweet earthy nose. Vanilla, mushrooms, wet earth and horse saddle.   A concentrated, sweet entrance into the mouth. Soaked currants in syrup, light sandalwood, cracked pepper and sweet spice kicking up in the end all the way up to the nostrils.   This is a near perfect for me. Paired with beef cheek.
Black currant grass herbal stewed cherries, plum marmalade vanilla nail polish, high acidity/tannin full body. Will be brilliant in a few years, very dominant tannins
Walkaround tasting so limited notes. Might have suffered from the tasting environment as my only notes are "service station toilet. Alcohol and tannin."
Strong, powerful, but very enjoyable texture. Need some time to open full potential but worth waiting. Beautiful match with red meats.
Amazing wine, unlike anything I’ve tried before, big on the palate, almost hard to describe. raisin chocolate cherry plum tobacco oak
Fruit forward fig good with everything light smooth
Strong and rich with a raisin flavor.
🌟🌟🌟🌟.2 Martignetti Grand Wine Experience industry Tasting. PnP.
Really good one!!! U MUST TRY!!
Enjoyed a glass in Verona
Fantastic. She will open up like a bouquet.
Lovely wine. Powerful , rich, spicy with high ABV yet easy to drink. Utterly wonderful.(2004)
Classic amarone, raisin, smoky, spice, mocha, plum, tannin is a bit tougher than expected
Tastes young. Heavy fruit. Raisin and savory flavors developed a bit more over 2 hours, but still a bit more fruit than I would prefer
Rich, luscious ripe fruit flavor with a complex fiery oak and tobacco finish
Nose: strong alcohol. Taste: leather, oak, ripe plum, cranberry. Raisin and Blackberry aftertaste. Smooth finish. Highly acid, dry tasting. Medium to full body.

Very different from what I am used to. Medium to medium full bodied. Tobacco, oak, plum, and a touch of earthy flavor hit the nose. Palate has cherry, red fruit, and a herbaceous note like dried basil. Finish had a bitterness to the likes of the aftertaste of a grapefruit. Grippy(ish) tannins. Delicious and different.

Very powerful. Very full bodied as expected from an Amarone. Nose of prune and dates and blackcurrant. A very distinct medicine nose and I got reminded of jagermister. Full bodied. So intense and powerful

Deep Ruby. Pronounced intensity of fresh and dried red fruit. Cherry, strawberry, prune, violets. Dry. Medium plus acidity, body, high alc,  pronounced intensity of fresh and dried fruit, cloves, violets. Medium plus ripe but firm Tannins. Medium plus Finish. Very good. Suitable for aging. Good concentration and mainly fresh fruit with strong structural elements like the very present tannins that will likely integrate better over time as well as further development of tertiary aromas.

4.2 *** this is quite delightful. Was a great compliment to NY style pizza with Chicago Italian sausage.  Deep ruby color with medium ruby rim and long, thin legs.  Nose of red fruit, dried leaves and brown sugar.  Opening of red fruit favoring cranberry with dark chocolate, brioche, fig and graphite.  Medium bodied and medium-plus acidity with an earthy, dusty tannin structure that lingers and fades slowly.  Peace and cheers!

The best amarone we had for this Italy trip. Bright Ruby red, what a bouquet on the nose! Very aromatic, floral notes of lavender, rose, berries on the palate, well balanced wine, honey notes, medium bodied, delicious with cheese.

This may be the smoothest  red I have ever had. First  sniff, there is an metallic hint, but after the first sip that is gone. It supposed to  be mixed berry, but it is so balanced no single berry stood out. Too easy to drink, will get a bottle  next time instead of glass. Definitely  added to my must have list.
Very nice blueberry pie and whiskey cocktail sorta
Much lighter and more pepper than a typical Amarone.  Decent wine but I’ve certainly had better ones.

Med garnet  Pronounced intensity Violet rose red cherry red plum raspberry black pepper liquorice rose raisin prune vanilla cedar toast leather tobacco coffee chocolate Developing  Off dry Med + acidity Full body High tannin High alcohol Pronounced intensity Long finish  Quality - outstanding Balance 1 Length 1 Intensity 1 Complexity 1  Drink now potential for ageing

A reliable and delicious Amarone. It’s warming, with welcome flavours of dark fruit, cherry, tobacco and leather. Great enjoyed now or in the next couple years. Silky tannins, nicely balanced. Will certainly buy this again.
Nose: leather, star anise and touch of vanilla with a spicy encompassing smell taste: tobacco, strawberry, pepper
Top Amarone but I had better for a better price. It’s good but not amazing.
Red fruit, oak, dried fruit.
Still not a fan of Amarones but this is actually good
Big tobacco, cigar, espressoanf fake chocolate with plum notes.
v out of my comfort zone but clearly an amazingly made wine
Dog food aromas. Must have with food.
Balsamic, coffee, plums, leather, tobacco
Nice wine! 2014 has a bouquet of  cherry with hints of  raisin
Really good, fruit on the nose, dry but enjoyable. Enjoyed in Glenwood at Trattoria
2019 is a good indication where this one could be heading
This is fantastic  raisin chocolate plum had it with my good friend Simon
Very fruit forward.  Lots of cherry and some toasted nuts
Boiled sweet vibes again.
Vin riche et gourmand. Sucre résiduel. Notes d'amertume.
Lavender, Violet, Blackberry, Black Plum, Chalky mouth feel, Medium Plus Tannin
Full bodied, deep notes of tobacco and plum. Yum yum
Pepper! Nearly tart blackberry , cherry. The 2013 can hold.
Very spicy, great backbone
Balanced but sweet, very much prunes, dried fruit, smooth but intense, unique! Bit porty.. yum. Went well with smokey sourdough pizza
Bought a half bottle, amazing big red wine. Complex in taste and nose
An absolute classic pair to a steak for me - this vine is delicious, full, lush, flavorful. A great love of mine.

Citrus, juicy, white peach, mineral. Very flavorful, not a porch pounder but would be good with creamy/mushroomy pastas, etc.

Delicate and aromatic nose of nectarines, citrus, and white flowers. The palate has a beautifully balanced fresh acidity, and remains light while having a broad mouthfeel/smooth mineral character that coats the mouth. Great stuff
One of the best examples of Lugana terroir. Aromatic but balanced by great acidity and saline finish
Wonderful light. Citrus notes with a light peach and white fruit.
Enoteca Japan. Very enjoyable
Stone fruit, pear drops, creamy but still fresh

Delicate apple pear taste, medium body, smooth finish and bronze colour

Straw yellow, perfect clarity. Notes of lemon peels, jasmine, bergamot. Flavors of citrus and strong acidity. Very fresh.
Great flower bouquet nectarine great with sushi
Very intense aromas of apple, pear, gooseberry, peach, elderberry, lychee, passion fruit, elderflower, jasmine, fresh grass. Light bodied, fresh, with long finish.
Felt like a chablis type with this stonefruit peach italian characteristics It the end good just a high amount of sulphites fornthe sensitive ones

Beautiful nose! Palate is good, too, but almost disappointing after the bouquet. Fresh flowers, lively acidity. Great with a slight chill. Moderate salinity and minerality makes me think it would be good with seafood or creamy pasta.
I was blown away. The style that I like. Powerful. Powerful aroma, taste, full body. Decent acidity. I could literally breeze it and chew it. Top! citrus pear minerals peach stone yeast bread yeast
So good. Crisp, cold, fruity. Perfect with out seafood in Venice
Like an odd crossing between an SB and a Gew? Pungently aromatic in all the right ways. Grassy. Fruity. Floral. Amazing.
This has all of the aromatics of German Riesling to me. Petrol, flowers and lemon on the nose, short finish, medium plus acidity, just ok but not what I expect from Turbiana
Florality, honeysuckle and pineapple on the nose. Smooth and crisp. Great wine and easy drinking
In a piazza in verona. Grass. Tropical fruit. Lemon. Nice acidity. Some residual creamy sweetness.
Creamy, stone, lime, pear drops
Flavour of citrus and peach. Aromatic, intense and dry in the end. Wonderfull!
Fresh and light.
big body, but not weighty ripe fruit, some earth on the nose Tons of semi-ripe tangelo, honeydew Grapefruit
Very delicate and subtle! Notes of pear... Crisp, but full of taste
Sweet and feels buttery - honeysuckle! Back porch drink.
Crisp and refreshing. Floral, peach, and apple notes.
Acidic and minerality. Great pairing with boiled shrimp and oil pasta
Peachy, mango, dry, bold. Quite good.
It’s aaaaaaight. Not the best cheap wine
Such a cool wine - lots of unique flavors. Rubber on the nose, grassy green pepper, hint of jalapeño. minerals stone
super crisp and light!
pear peach lemon
Not as nice as the grosjean Petit. Nutty to smell. Not as fresh as grosjean but acidity due to lake Garda
Loved this. Crisp but more interesting than Sauvignon bland. Had at fin boys with Neil 14 Sept 2023
Min absolutte favorit

A good friend served this white with a specialty smoked bbq pork chop dinner last evening. It’s been another of those many local Italian grapes I’ve never heard of before, Turbania, with a small % of Cortese which I have heard of!  A golden colour with a fragrant nose and complimentary aromas of ripe lychee, pear, apricot/peach, lemon zest  and honeysuckle. The mouth feel hints of slight oily taste on the tongue for a tasty body texture that lingers on the palate creating a demand for ‘another glass please’.

Pale lemon color with aromas of stone fruit, citrus and floral notes. On the palate flavors of apple, pear and peach with citrus and nutty notes. Good acidity, balance and structure, medium+ finish ending with salty mineral character.

Love this.  The nose is peachy hay, sea air and spice with a lime twist. Perhaps a bit of tomato leaf?  Savoury straw palate, citric lime (med) acidity with a bit of the tropics. Saline minerality, citrus zest and gingery salted butter.   Rounded smooth mouthfeel, good body.  Long ripe finish.

Happy DWTHYW Saturday. A ⛄️ and 🐧 choice for honey garlic turkey meatballs and all the veggie fixings honeyed.  Just 2️⃣ more sleeps till 🎅   Trusted palates Red and Emcee guided me here with their red noses 😛.  Oh wait that’s Rudolph 🦌 🎅 🛷   First vintage of trusted Allegrini playing with Turbiana 🍇 from its home in Lugana.   Lovely white with a tangerine / passionfruit dream ( very Sb like, but more creamy and acidity though just medium ), salinity and a beauty aromatic start add to the interest. Yum 🤤
Aromatic, freshness, herbal touch. Fine acidity, precise aromas and well balanced. A great example of a Lugana as it is meant to be
3.8/5.0 Lugana often is a good choice.  Mineral, white flowers, pear, jasmine, lime, cream. Rounded acidity, med.+body. Resume: rather very good
Light golden color, Less acidity, moderate oily  citrus minerals   almond oil   pear peach stone   cream  Great pair with oysters and even steak
Apple, pear, wax, honey, almonds on the nose. Cream, pear, apricots on the palate. Awesome wine like a cross between a chenin blanc and chard
I feel the high quality of the gorgeous scent and calm coolness. Aromas such as green apples, pears, and kiwis are impressive.
Good Lugana freshness and balanced acidity. On the nose aromas of citrus minerals lemon stone and similar mouthfeel with good medium finish.
Liked it a lot. Stone fruits esp peaches. Nice crispness,  mineralogy/stone. Fruity but also some complexity.  111 Bistro 4-25
Beautifully fresh, light, fragrant.  Rose, peach, lemon, pear lime white peach jasmine passion fruit.  Gorgeous and value for money
Notes of melon, papaya, cucumber, lemon, sea salt. Light body, acidic, long finish. Would pair well with a creamy chicken dish.
On the nose 👃 big peach, apricot and hints of honeysuckle   To taste 👅 citrus flavour of lime, peach, mango, crisp green apple. Great length on the finish.
Absolute beauty! First breath was of mango and pineapple. Bright citrus.  Red is my go-to, but I Truly loved this bottle. Brought summer to life at the end of January.
Hints of peach, butter, and lemon. Very smooth. Low to tannins. Easy to drink. Will definitely have it again! This is superb! Considering that the wine is young
A 2022 vintage drank in 2024.  🍇 Turbiana & Cortese  Alc. 13%  Country: Italy  A nice bottle of wine. Apples, citrus and hintanof stone fruits. Medium plus acidity.
Lovely wine, dangerously easy to drink! Low acidity, light colour with notes of peach, apricot and a floral finish. Pairs beautifully with seafood.
This beautiful white smells of lemon zest, unripe apple and pear, with elderflower and flint notes. In the mouth it’s medium bodied, high acidity, and with a fresh long finish. Banger!
Complex nose full of citric and saline notes, high minerality, peach, palate is deep and almost balanced although with acidity slightly higher than Lugana should have, imo
Great white from Veneto. Aromas of passion fruit, mango, lemon, asparagus, grass, green bell pepper. Paired perfectly with slices of crude salmon with lemon and serrano chili.
Lugana freshness captured. A good white showing clean citrus and stone fruit notes. The palate offers nice freshness with balanced minerality. Good length throughout.[DFWE 2024]
pear citrus minerals peach lemon lime stone grapefruit white peach apricot pineapple apple grass almond stone fruit jasmine melon nectarine yellow apple vanilla saline green almond kiwi biscuit
Impressed by light green color, nose of peach, honey, green apple and passion fruit. Medium body, dry , medium acidity with moderate finished. Pairing with seafood plate.
Very floral on Jose, but dry yet a good bit of fruit to taste. Rolling notes of apple and pair with long finish. Good choice out to Italian seafood with rick and Phil in Travisa!!

My first Lugana and this is a beauty! From around Lake Garda in N Italy. This is 96% Turbiana. Short stainless ferment and ageing. Nice 13%.   White peach 🍑, minerals, white florals, almond, lemon. Subtle minerals. Really bright.   Summer sunshine in a glass and a new intrigue for me.   Med body, med+ acid, some bitterness, dry.   Will be happy to find more.   👍🇮🇹😎

An interesting white picked up in Sidney BC today.  This wine poured a clear gold with lingering legs.  The nose clean medium intensity with notes of peach hint of citrus and mineral this is developed well.  The mouth, dry, medium plus acidity a little less alcohol. Medium plus body and long finish.  The peach and mineral notes predominate. The mouth feel here is extrodinary with clearly some oak in the process. It's drinking well now and in balance, it could age a bit yet too

A perfect pairing with spaghetti frutta di mare, this cuvée is made of 96% Turbiana and 4% Cortese hand harvested grapes from a southwest facing vineyard at the shores of Lake Garda with clay soils. Fermented and aged for about 4 months in steel tanks.  Light hay yellow colour with medium thick tears. Ripe green apples, tones of peaches, orange blossoms and spices, hints of lemons and elderberries, crispy minerality, some almonds in the long refreshing finish.

Great white enjoyed at Andreas Huahin! Lovely company tonight. Nose of fresh citrus, white blossom, sweet basil, and minerals. Full body, M+ acidity, lengthy finish. Palate of tropical, mango, persimmon, white peach, hint of stone, grass, almond. Very pleasurable on multitude of citrus and green fruits. Minerals baseline supports the whole palate. Splendid wine.

Best Lugana I have had so far, though it’s not my piece of cake. Fruity, round, pear, melon, some peach. The greatest thing about this particular Lugana is that it has the right level of acidity and freshness. Makes it way more pleasant and vibrant. Quite a lot of alcohol though, but at least the acidity compensates a bit. If you go Lugana, then opt for this instead of the Ca dei Frati’s of this world. I still don’t get the popularity of Lugana though, I guess it’s the easiness, sweetness and fruitiness.

Very light. Promiscuous on the tongue. Vibrant. - Wes “Very nice acid. Light on the palate. Coats the mouth delicately” - Andrew “Simple but elegant. Heavy yet smooth. I don’t know anything about wine. Gets the job done” - Ethan “A sweet Earth. Like a sweet grass” - Hannah

Lugana 2023. 13% abv  Medium straw.  Freshly cut grass, camomile, grapefruit, green pear give a greenish nose.  Similar on the palate, mainly citrus fruits and green herbs. Salty minerality. Medium long finish. Light body.  Fresh summer wine. ⭐ 3.9

Second approach to the wine. Upgrading to 4 stars. Was able to feel a distinct taste of peach and plum.  I like wines that can be remembered for smth. This here is a mix of local varieties - cortese and turbiana grapes (turbiana is a variety of verdicchio). The wine still drinks easy - very low on minerals and acidity.

Beautiful, fresh and light with crisp tropical fruit flavours. Notes of pineapple, honeydew melon and peach. Well balanced on the palate. Slight zingy acidity. Dry, elegant finish. Has a quality NZ Sauvignon Blanc feel.

I wanted to give it a 4.2. I succumbed to the herd mentality.  Starts extremely slow. After an hr or so of air, as expansive as a acid based wine can be. Blind tasted, I would have guessed Grand Cru Chablis. Well done.

Lovely Lugana from this Veneto house of trust. Turbiana (96%) and Cortese (4%). Aging in stainless steel tanks for 4 months. Intense citrus aromas with grapefryit, lemon zest, gooseberry and passion fruit. Grassy, crispy on the palate with a lively acidity. Medium+ finish. 90-91 pts. Excellent Lugana.

Wine festival 4-29-23 Wine #2 First vintage of this for Allegrini!  They did it right!  Best white at the event! Pale lemon color.  Super fresh aromas of citrus and flowers.  Palate is tangerine, white flowers, white peach, and wet stones. This is what a Lugana should taste like! Dry with medium+ body and acidity.  13% alcohol

Strogeel met in de neus tropisch fruit en mineraal. In de mond appel, perzik, citrus, ziltig, amandel en mineraal. Medium+ body en medium+ afdronk. — Straw yellow with tropical fruit and mineral in the nose. In the mouth apple, peach, citrus, salty, almond and mineral. Medium+ body and medium+ aftertaste.

Very good wine! This has concentration, character and length. Fresh and round wine, with a lovely racy acidity. Blend of 96% Turbiana (local name for Verdicchio) and 4% Cortese, aged in steel tanks for 4 months. Medium+ intense aromas of white peach, apricot, lime, mandarin, blossom, biscuit, white pepper, wet stone. Dry wine, medium+ acidity, medium body, medium+ flavour intensity, long finish.  13% - €11,95 @ Vivino

First aroma is passion fruit, elderflower, lime. Herbal character reminds me of better NZ Sauvignon. Fresh, stone character with lasting acidity. Since visiting Garda I always try to find the best Lugana, this is one of them, especially considering the price.

Drank in 25’. Bright crisp pear and citrus on the nose. Very refreshing with peach and the notes on the nose hit the tongue. Some minerality on the end. Very refreshing and would absolutely drink again.

My final try from when I was at waterford wine club. Orange, lemon, peach and a little creaminess to it. A solid bottle, dont think Ive had this grape before Turbiana. (Update I had the 2022 two years ago) Also with decent amounts of acidity.  90 points

Wow really god, better then expected, mouthwatering. 👀 Medium yellow 👃 White peach, fresh green salad, lemon 👅 Medium plus body, god structure,medium plus bitter medium acidity  👄 Grape, green salad, lemon and minerals

Nice full bodied, aromatic wine. Aromas of crisp green apple, lemongrass, and seashells. On the palate: bright, tart acidity and almonds on the finish. Would be good for someone who likes a fruity New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc.

Light yellow color with greenish accents in the glass! On the nose scents of lemon mint, lime and almond blossom with a hint of camomile! On the palate racy attack, fresh with a juicy  and refreshing finish! Cheers!

A pretty solid bottle of Lugana, opened after a hard day to relax and drink some wine. Nothing particularly remarkable, but overall a decent white. Its main problem is that it’s too mineral, which prevents the flavors from fully unfolding.

whatever I drank from family owned Allegrini was delicious, does’t matter if it was budget Soave or higher shelf Amarone. This Trebbiano is no exclusion, just shows in a middle shelf wine can taste good. Abundance of aromas & flavours (cistrus and stone fruits, jasmine), bit more body, balance and wrapping all this fruity core good med+ refreshing acidity with lemon / grapefruit zest medium finish. Mild but zesty that’s yin/yan balance. Damn good stuff in a warm day! Cheers

Pale lemon in color with a youthful medium minus intensity nose. Citrus, lemon, grapefruit, tropical, mango, peach, pear. Dry, high acid, medium minus body, medium alcohol and medium intensity on the body. Long finish. Grapefruit, mango, citrus, peach, pear. Outstanding quality of wine. Drink now.

With lugana a certain standard is guaranteed! Had a nice development initially pointing to some barrel-like notes but gradually more fruity aromas came forward while minerals and stone were evident in the background. Acidity was well balanced and the closing remained pleasant and refreshing. Nose hinted pear cream apricot and similar hues. Excellent VfM. A 3.9

Pale straw yellow in color with greenish reflections. Elegant aromas of candied lemon peel, honey suckle, yellow apple and chamomile blossoms are introduced in the beguiling nose. The delicate , understated fragrances proffer floral notes and hints of toasted almonds. The palate is graceful , well- defined , taut and flavorsome!

A blend of Turbiana (96%) & Cortese (4%), varieties unknown to me until now. Intense aromas of citrus, lemon, lime, pear, peach, apricot, honeysuckle and jasmine. Refreshing in the mouth with mild sense of minerals and wet stone, medium acidity and also medium aftertaste. 👌
Fresh and light-bodied, with lively blood orange and raspberry fruit flavors accented by hints of chive blossoms, stone, smoke and grapefruit peel.
Aroma of citrus, peach, pear. Taste of lemon, lime, stone, minerals. Medium bodied, medium acidity, dry.
Dry, medium body and clear notes of citrus lime, mango, pear and cream. Balanced, yet complex and fun.
Rach: had at turnips in borough market. Easy drinking, refreshing, light summer white wine. Crowd pleaser.
Stone fruit and white flower on the nose. Citrus and white peach in the palate. High acid and nice viscosity.
Aromas of peach and pineapple. Lemon and grapefruit flavors with a clean finish. Nice balance of acid and fruit.
Love Lugana citrus pear minerals peach lemon lime stone grapefruit white peach cream apricot pineapple apple
Great alternative for SB from NZ. Riper fruit such as white peach, grapefruit, with rounder acidity.
Pretty good Italian White - Fresh, light. Notes of citrus, lime, crispy clear golden colors. Looking forward to a repeat!
[0.5oz tasting] Sweet aromas of honeydew, melons, peaches. Light body. Bright, crisp and refreshing. Surprisingly nice.
citrus pear minerals- very drinkable Italiano - smooth and great With light pasta. Dishes . Marshall house 2024
Notes of grass, nettle and gooseberry on the nose. Smooth on the palate with light acidity and fruit notes.

RL=4.3 VV=4.5 🇮🇹🇮🇹🇮🇹 100%Corvina Veronese   Alegrini's family is the leader of the Valpolicella Classic Style. The reasons are: more than 520 years dedicated to the vineyard development in it's more than 100 hectares and its different drying processes.  👁Opaque Deep Ruby 👃Fig, leather & earthy  💋Jam of red fruit, Tobacco, sweet raisins, Dark chocolate w big grains & Velvety tannins  👚Robust  💫 Marked by its Velvety tannins, & dried fruit. ⏰Very long aftertaste
Another one brought by a colleague. This one is a lot oak leather tobacco chocolate With prune raisin plum.
Dried Plum, Black Cherry, Fig, Light Earthy, Medium Plus Tannin, Chalky mouth feel
4.3 Nice surprise. You won’t be disappointed!
Refined and elegant
Vintage 2009, notes to come.
oak, earthy, tea leaves, balsamic vinegar, chocolate, black plum, very good acidity with smooth tannin and long finish! 100 percent Corvina, made in amarone style!

Big Fat Italian Wine  This one is heavy and smooth at the same time, it’s rich and intense in aromas with a deep dark color, balanced complexity with chocolate and tobacco notes, bouquet of dark fruit.

With a lovely port nose of old oak, dried plums, deep earth, lovingly used leather with hints of rosemary this wine is a delight! A spicy body with floatingly velvety tannins and softly balanced acidity my palette is dancing!

A wine full deserving of 5 stars I think! La Poja is the luxury wine of Allegrini - there’s just shy of 8000 bottles made and the 2015 is their current vintage.   Made with 100% Corvina this has an intense smoke and game nose with loads of cherry - yet it’s still holding onto some freshness.  It’s rich with thick tannins that have softened - it’s had 2 years in new oak.   It’s one of those wines that you drink slowly because the length just stays with you. This is a decadent wine that feels like a treat.
Elegant aroma. Chocolate. Tobacco.  Blackberry. High acid.

One of my italian favorites! An elegant wine, let it breathe and it will come out excellent. Earthy, well-balanced, oak, Chocolate and dark plum. Some tobacco and dark cherry. Strong and delicious!! All-time-favorite ❤️
Full, mineral, rooty. Possible bad pour from an old bottle. Unpleasant but well reviewed. Skeptical. Had at Sale Pepe in Lahaina
Smooth finish not overly fruity with a splash of pepper.
Another one n Tappo with Tatys. Amazing wine
Lovely smooth
Really enjoyed this wine (Marcus) Smooth and improved as it opened up

On the nose features notes of leather and smoke with strong wood oak notes and subtle vanilla. In taste plum is present and blackberries come forward. Very strong tannins as well so decanting would be beneficial.
Excellent, like drinking a cordial, only you want more after the first sip. Open an hour before dinner and you will love it
Intense fruit on the nose, rich taste of dark fruit and berries . One of the best wines from Allegrini!
Quite intense but also fragrant , shows that Corvina when well handled can produce great red wine
Very fruity and smooth
Super strong. It needs beef or a tomato based ragout. Otherwise, it tastes like refuse.
rotten fruit wrapped in leather

Superfly (Curtis Mayfield) You know the term Super Tuscan? Well this is a Super Veneto! Made from 100% Corvina Veronese this dark red currant in color, had a bit of a subdued nose but compensated that in the mouth with a full-bodied cherries, blackberries, plums, herbs and licorice. Great depth, beautifully balanced, velvety tannins, a fresh acidity and a lingering come-back of cherries in the finish. If you like Amarone or Ripasso, you should definitely try this Super-wine!

A new 🇮🇹 beauty that sells at £62-£92 (crazy price differences) that’s so silky smooth it made me want to wax my bottom 🤪 I was expecting boom but got oooooo & arrrrrrr 😃  💥 14.5% ABV  🍇 Corvina  🍷 Deep opaque ruby  👃 Spiced blackberry & currant, blueberry & red cherry w/ soft smoked oak, raisin, leather, liquorice, chocolate goo & coffee  📝 more .. 👇

La Poja, monoverietal Corvina Veronese is a pure Veneto treasure. The 2008 vintage needed an hour to shake off its baby appearance and reveal fermented sour cherries, wild strawberry, clear ,focused fruit granite rock,leather, sour plum developing to plum liqueur. Dried prunes. Grippy mouthfeel with still young tannins. They support the fruit and spice beautifully.  The tannins will take another decade to soften and reveal sweet spice and dried fruit . Superb effort from Allegrini. Bravo.

An elegant red, with an enticing nose of ripe fruit and floral accents, as well as a tang of iron-laced minerality that underscores flavors of ripe black raspberry and cherry fruit, star anise and Earl Grey tea. Medium- to full-bodied and well-knit, sleek, fine-grained tannins emerge to firm the lasting finish. Hard to stop sipping, but should expand nicely with even short-term cellaring. Drink from 2025.

And my tasting goes on, and, as well this wine will hit my cellar! This deep and dark (black) wine with a nice ruby corner shows a wonderful nose of red fruits and berries, red cherry, blackberry, leather! Fantastic, the palate is not getting disappointed, a round, fleshy, medium deep bodied wine, nice red and black fruits, wood, leather, white pepper, wow... Medium long aftertaste. Italian bomb ...👍🏻😋🍷

Undeniably very high quality mono Corvina aged 20m in 100% new oak, it’s bold, powerful & opulent but w. stylish elegance too.  Med ruby. Lovely pronounced aroma intensity w. ripe raspberry, cherry, black cherry, cassis & cedar. Vanilla & nutmeg w. liquorice & clove. Dark chocolate. Vibrant & expansive.  Dry. Very ripe, plush & fruity, vibrant w. juicy, rich black cherry, blackcurrant, creamy raspberry, damson & blackberry. Intense & concentrated w. vanilla & cocoa. M+ fine tannins, m+ alc, m+ acid & a long finish.

This is absolutely banging, I feel slightly bad I'm not sharing it with any of you. Deep garnet. Intense nose of red cherry, rose, raisin and spice. Lovely freshness, oddly more floral than my last bottle in 2015. The palate adds chocolate, tobacco and herbs, liquorice and coffee. Amarone style, bitterness, 5g/l residual sugar. Full body, 14.5% ABV, smooth lower tannins, high acidity. It improves over 3+ hours so plenty of life in it yet. Superb with braised beef short ribs and mushrooms this evening.

The first Corvina only wine I am trying. Very elegant and smooth. Allegrini delivers again ,-) Red fruits, lots of forest floor and animal and leather notes, good acidity and lovely mouthfeel. Like the smooth tannins.
It's a great wine, a little bit expensive, but still great. Intense fruit taste, medium body and medium acidity. Highly recommend
Aromas of honey, blackberry, and leather invite to this acidic and tannic red thats fills the palate with flavors of leather & black currant
93 pts. Deep purple black cherry blueberry mulberry a bit of white truffle and milk chocolate full body with soft silky tannins and long finish.
An excellent flowery, slightly floral fruit, earthyness, wonderful aging. Nice balance and very soft tannins. Drink it tday - will not improve!
My favorite 100% Corvina Veronese. A bit strong in the beginning. I should have let it breath more. Notes of dark chocolate, coffee and old oak.
20 years later and this wine is still delicious.  Dark purple with concentrated dark fruits of blackberry and plum with earthy undertones
Den beat me to it. Same marks, same opinion. Pretty damn mellow and fruity, well balanced, goes wellwith meat and brie cheese
Leather and black fruits. Pretty high acidity, smooth tannins and an amazing long finish. Amazing wine, nothing like any Amarone.
What an exciting wine. Pure Corvina creating a very well defined and long velvety tongue,  with a nice touch of herbs and a lingering spicyness.
The summit of corvina. Very aromatic in nose. Great weight in mouth. Vibrant an spicy. Long finish with excellent delineation.
Unlike any other that I tasted at the Laithwaites vintage wine festival. Raisin, cherry and almond. Luxurious and silky smooth.
Very balanced and smooth. high concentration. Strawberry, sour cherrys, some vanilla and spices, leaves. Elegant with long finish. Still alive!
Speechless! 4.7. Compared to 2008 it is a bit softer in tannins and more balanced. Still full of power . A beatiful experience.
animal sweat, dried cherry, soy sauce, wet leaves, caramel, high grippy tannins, full body, medium acidity. nose of old wines
Coffee, nuance of damp forest. With time, cigarettes, beautiful acid and red fruit taste comes out, unique and interesting taste.
Been a while old friend. Wip
Very nice corvina, velvet on the tongue and strong blackberry flavor. Quite complex smell, front taste and back taste are apart and intriguing.
Dark fruit oak licorice vanilla raisin on the nose full body very firm tannins high accidity accidity dark fruit cedar licorice oak earthy hint of cinnamon long finish
Sneaky magnum night changed to Thursday.... So I changed the vintage! 2000 so smooth and enjoyable ... A well structured , round and full fruit wine...
Wonderful Covina.  Dark fruits especially plum with chocolate and oak.  Well balanced with a smooth finish that continued to open up throughout the evening.  Delicious!
Another great La Poja from the 1998 vintage. Full body, still with some fruit and some tannins. Drink now or hold for another year. Very good bottle. 100% Corvina
Red and dark fruit, jam of plumes, macerated cherries, Provençal herbs, sweet spices, dark chocolate. Silky and sweet tannins with a gorgeous finish.
A new style for me. Hints of petrol on the nose and almost quite French, a bit of melon and quite a sweet entree. Blackcurrant and deep body. Great drink
Mature and ready. Complex bouquet of dried cherries, ripe plum, chocolate and spices. Full body, 14.5, pretty balanced, medium acidity, delicious long finish.
A Five Star wine! La Poja ..from the Veneto region..Monovarietal Corniva Veronese...plummy aromas forest floor spices...spectacular palate.. Love it very much! Salud!!🍷🍷
If you have Gorgonzola or any other powerful taste that matches this monster … I bought it shortly after release, for a fraction of today price.. my rating is based on much lower pricing
Rich, full & complex. Ripe fruit, cassis, damson and cherry, sweet spices and notes of cured meat, leather and a whiff of pipe tobacco Very moreish
93/100  Really delicate aroma of ripe cherry and plum, white pepper, tabacco, anise and even leather. Rich body, with chocolate and vanilla finish. Full and spicy.   #nekislo777
Like a broken record I would like to repeat that this is not a wine, it is nectar with strong flavors of plum, dark and sweet cherry, chocolate, black currant.. perfect wine, my favorite one!
Nice aroma of cherry, blackberry and  tobacco. Full bodied, powerful but not too masculine. On the palate notes of blackberry, plum balanced with vanilla, chocolate and oak.
Cracking wine! Cloudy and opaque but after 30 mins in a decanter this was delicious. The sommelier recommended this and I was so glad he did. It was also excellent value. Top wine
Rich well-balanced corvina\nA rare example of singlegrape recent wine producing such a pronounced flavour!\nHad game heavy meal and this bottle kept enough body to be well enjoyed
Tobacco, chocolate, raisin, riped blackberry and smoky notes. H tannin, M acidity, F body and long finish.  Good with or without food. Balanced, sweet, deep and dark. Superb.  4.3+
1997–Fantastic single vineyard corvina veronese....a little past its prime but still very good...mature red fruit aromas -forest fooor ...if you have one drink it now! Salud!!🍷🍷
Coconut, dark chocolate, coffee, almonds, raisin, violet, touch of sweetness. Corvina is overwhelmingly pleasant. As powerful as any Amarone. Coconut plus raisin = corvina
nose of blackberry, black cherry , oak and some tertiary flavours such as leather . Medium tannin and medium acidity. Very easy drinking and complex. BLIC Rating of 4, superb wine
Great! Verona gives us perfect medium body and well balanced tannins/acidity example. Tobacco, spices, oak and finally chocolate on the background. 1hr decant could make it better only. Do not miss!
One of my favorite IGT wine in italy.Dry,Mid ruby intensive primary cherry,plum follows toasty vanilia and smoke med acidity med body silky tannin kong finish quite elegant summer wine

This was super good. Not natural, but well made and balanced for a 14.5% wine. You could call it a Super Venetian wine or a baby Amarone. Whatever the name, cracking with some meat. Not sure I would buy again for the price, but was definitely a crowd pleaser. Red fruit, leather, soft tannins, super long length

100% Corvina Veronese from one of the most drunken Veneto producers from my own behalf.  It has a subtle dried fruit character that makes the wine more rich, sweet and round aroma compounds. Even tho it isn't too much of anything.  It still carries a lush and elegance flavor that is easy to fall deeply into.  Dried fruit, raspberries, floral and moisty elements. Well recommend.

Wow this is what Corvina can be!!  Unlike the raison style intense Amarone this La Poja 2018 is surgical and elegant.   The nose we get black cherry, graphite, and dried herbs. On the palate, it’s remarkably fresh but w/powerful silky tannins carry layers of dark chocolate, espresso, and a distinct flinty minerality from that limestone plateau it is on.  Just also had the 2016 which was a 4.6 with a much more stupendous nose!!   I normally do not like Allegrini. Take this and skip their Amarone. This is amazing!!!

Deep, intense a extraordinarily fruity Corvina , it embodies the maximum finesse that the noble Veronese grape can express in a treasure trove of opulence, tautness and vitality. It’s aromatic profile includes rich flavors of black currants, dark cherry pulp, fig cake, menthol, milk chocolate truffles and savory undertones of cured tobacco, undergrowth and worn leather . The focused flavor range provides flesh for the resolved tannins, echoing on the impressively lasting finish!

A wonderfully complex medium body red from northern italy. The first aromas that hit you are sour cherry and chocolate. After a while, tobacco, balsamic and leather start to weave their way. Even though the alcohols are on the higher side, the wine remains quite elegant. The finish lasts for minutes though. Velvety tannins and med+ acidity show the true strength of this great wine.

Pure Corvina, not made Amarone style.  Bold, extremely well-structured, seductive. Still in the fruit phase but equipped with enormous aging potential.  Single vineyard.   I like this much better than 99% of Amarone.

Bottle No.07 @ Enoteca Wine Taste  Never have this aroma before which also gives me a wow factor with unique flavor on taste and smell. Well balance lovely and sexy red.😘  Very rich, smokey, leathery and fruity on nose with hint of dark cherry plum & blue berry. Smooth to drink with unique medium-dry sweetness with refreshing acidity on palette. Velvety tannins with good long sexy finish. 🍷  Is it a Buy, don't Buy or I don't know?  My Result > Is a sure buy 🍷 4.5🌟  My Wife Result > Is a buy🍷 4.5🌟

Deep ruby. Pronounced sweet red fruit red cherry plum violet rose grass clove toast. Dry high acidity high tannin smooth full body. Delicious sweet fruit well integrated oak. Long length. Outstanding.

A beautiful elegant wine, since it is made with 100% Corvina, it can only be an IGT wine, but a glorious one at that, opaque burgundy color with nose of black plum, smoke, charcoal, charcuterie, dried fig, red earth, portobello mushroom, and hibiscus, full body with balanced acidity and refined tannin, drinking beautifully with tastes of prune, date, black plum, hibiscus, toasted almond, cedar, cacao, raspberry, dried cranberry, fig, garrique, clove, star anise cacao and roasted chestnut, super long finish.

Nose of strawberry and leather. Very dark ruby color almost inky in darkness. Molds acidity and soft, full mouthfeel with a hint of smoke, blackberry, stone. Pleasant feel of tannins with a peppery finish

Opaque purple high viscosity, earth, leather and cedar on the nose.  Soft mouthfeel initially that quickly explodes with fig, dark fruit, and a hint of anise that introduces the soft tannins that escort you to the end…which lingers and lingers until the next sip.  Wow!  An amazing wine even at this price point.

This changed my perspective on Corvina. No apassimento. Less than full bodied, plush fruit that is a little overripe, sweet spice like liquorice, tannins still present after ten years. Nice acidity and a finish of olives. Warm with a hint of sweetness. Could be confused for a syrah or malbec in a blind tasting.

Happy last day of #MIDI! 🇮🇹 Finishing off with this big complex beauty. Fragrant aromas of cassis, black cherry, leather, tobacco & cedar. Flavors of blackberry, dark cherry, raisin, chocolate, cinnamon, pepper & licorice. Lengthy finish of mocha & clove. Dry, med+ body and med+ acidity with supple tannins. Impeccably balanced with rich silky layers across a smooth mouthfeel. Vibrant acidity with intense yet elegant depth yields a wonderfully sophisticated palate. 100% Corvina. Deep ruby color. Excellent quality!

Last time Stig offered this beauty on Christmas Eve, now it is Eastern and it is just as beautiful... Thanks   A complex taste and aroma but the most prominent are oak tobacco blackberry chocolate and  prune. However many more apparent

4.3pts. Allegrini La Poja Monovitigno Corvina Veronese 2010. 100% Corvina Veronese. Although it is a completely different process, this La Poja is usually very popular with people who like Amarone. Drinking La Poja will give you a taste of Corvina. It is certainly a thick but delicate flavor like the frames that make up Amarone. Aroma from plum, raisin, prune, black cherry, blackcurrant, dried fruit, anise, truffle, licorice, leather and chocolate.

I barely don’t drink this kind of wine but man this is sooo seductive juicy and at the same beautifully balanced and never too fat and lacy, the black currant leather is to die for, look at my latest review on this for further

A fantastic wine made of 100 % Corvina! Dark red colour. Dark berries, spice and oak on nose. Full bodied, strong tannins but yet well balansed in mouth. After a few hours of decanting flavours came out beautifully. Cherries, blackberries, chocolate and some spice. Long aftertaste.

Intense, oak-tinged minerals hit the nose, accompanied by ripe plum and berry and hints of flowers. Full-bodied and chewy, with big, velvety tannins and fresh acidity that enhances the fruit flavors, leading to a long, well-balanced finish. Corvina. Best after 2008. W.S.92/100

Bought this at the Allegrini estate in Fumane, Verona in 2020 (higly recommended visit) . 100% Corvina Veronese, single vineyard, late harvest which gives some sweetness to the wine. 20 months on oak and barriques. Intense ruby red. Red fruit and spices in the nose. Full bodied with looong aftertaste. 93 points

This specialty single vineyard offering has it all. 100% Corvina. Beautiful dark ruby coloring. Full body, rounded tannins. Medium acidity. Black fruit such as fig, mixed berry pie, blackberry. Baking spices and chocolate faintly at the finish. Cedar and anise in between. If you visit Allegrini in the Veneto region, make sure to pick this up or the little brother (also excellent) La Grola.

Blackberries is all I can think about at first sniff. Opens up to more red fruit and a hint of oak and leather. Firm with good structure and an otherwise very pleasant mouth feel. Long finish with even more dark berries and coffeebeans.

Medium+ intensity with deep ruby redness. Nose is profound with rich black fruit notes, chocolate, oak, and hints of rose hip. Medium acidity and silk medium tannins carry this medium bodied wine. Mouth reflects nose though tobacco notes are more prominent as is the black fruit induced bitterness. Some cherry and herbaceousness comes through as well. Very complex and opens up beautifully as it breathes. Superb wine!

Came close to drinking 3 times over the years I had stored it, I’m glad we waited for it to peak. It is smooth silky and sexy. What can I say - the corvina veronese delivers cherry galore deepened with perfumed oak and fresh tobacco. Not as alcoholic as Allegrini’s Amarone, this is my preferred product from this beautiful winery. 94pts

A dream called La Poja - 100% Fresh Corvina - Fresh nose with a balsamic ending, beautiful red fruit, elegant, smooth tannins, allot influence from lake Garda, La Poja is a Cru in the Cru la Geda. Limestone underground with a touch of calcium

Nice Corvena wine , impressed by ruby color, nose of chocolate, oak, plum and tobacco. Silky smooth tannin on palate, quite sweet ,medium+ body, low acidity and moderate finished.Love her, recommended.

Limpid, ruby red, consistent. Intense, complex, leather, plum, mushroom, tobacco. Dry, warm, quite soft. Fresh, tannic and tasty. Full body +, balanced, intense, persistent. Ready with potential to evolve.

One of the best wines I have ever tasted in my almost 75 years and 60 yrs of wine tasting out of witch 40 yrs reallaly high quality wine tasting. Maybe the best wine ever, where Chateaux Latour was one of the top wines, maybe beaten by this. Stored for many, many years in my warm cellars. Toffee, nuts, raisins, tobacco, fudge, nougate, endless taste. How come, that high quality wines can be so extremely complex after many years?

Dark chocolate, ripe cherry,and notes of oaky vanilla. Such amazing purity of fruit. Quite tannic at this point. Long and succulent. I've always loved the clean and pure fruit in the wines from Allegrini. Kudos.

It is intense and complex, with a rich, full-bodied character and firm tannins. On the nose, it offers aromas of black cherry, blackberry, and plum, along with notes of vanilla, cinnamon, and toasted oak. On the palate, the wine is densely packed with flavors of dark fruit, chocolate, and espresso, with a hint of savory herbs. The tannins are firm and well-structured, providing a long, lingering finish. It has a great aging potential and will continue to improve with additional time in the bottle

Almost perfect. 12yrs o’bottle age bringing lovely leather to the party, alongside dried herbs, sage, oregano, sour cherry, smoky clove, lovely thick grainy tannins on the backbone … corvina delivering the goods - I would think fully mature at this point
March, '18. Best Prosecco I've had. 8-9 grams of sugar, so in the middle of brut.   At an industry tasting of luscious wines.
Very lively bubbles. A hard working Prosecco with no excessive sweetness or acidity. Definite melon on the palate and fantastic on the hot day it was.
I'm not crazy about their Pinot Grigio but this was a really solid Prosecco. Dry, refreshing, light fruits, a little toast. I can't recall drinking one nicer than this one.

A well above average Prosecco that delivers in all the right areas. A great mousse that continues to roll over the tongue with each sip. Clean, fresh & zippy without being too acidic. Plenty of taste on the palette without being too commanding over the previous finer points. Very nice, would definitely recommend.

Pear, peach, red apple, pie crust, hazelnut, chalk and lime on the nose. Dry, medium alcohol and acidity. Stone fruit, crushed stone, grapefruit and lime on the palate. Great complexity for a prosecco.

Woke up this morning to find this bottle on the kitchen island, accompanied by a gracious note from our most recent air bnb guest. Of course, the gesture is too kind, though in truth I’m truly looking forward to enjoying this wine. Their PG is a guilty pleasure of mine, and the Prosecco... well... 🥴🥂  Cheers friends!

2025: WSET Diploma D4 Sparkling tasting #10  Pale lemon, large beads, frothy.   Med+ aromas of lemon, apple skin, yellow plum, grassy, fennel.   Med- body, med+ acidity, med+ flavors of apple and lemon, pear, with a slatey finish.   Very good, better than I remember.  2024: A nice enough Prosecco, yellow apple, pear, nectarine with a fine mousse, residual sugar adding some body and a wet stone finish.

Pale straw. Aromas of green apple, ripe pear, and white peach, acacia blossom and a hint of citrus zest. On the palate, it’s crisp and refreshing, with lively bubbles. A versatile refreshing sparkler is perfect as an aperitif
Happy 2021!! nice green apple and pear aromas, balanced acidity with a little bread crust bitterness
Well done fizzy prosecco! Fruity with nice mix of pear, peach & apple notes....light, easy drinking & crisp. Enjoyable to drink as alone. Cheers! 🥂🍾
Green apple flavor with stone notes. Dry (++), acid (++++) and light body, very smooth (+). Alcohol (++). Pound to pound a nice option for it price.
Yellow color, it's really good at mouth!! Fruity!!  Notes of citrus fruit!!! And the bubble it's beautiful and continue!! Really good for a prosecco
A great wine despite being from a giant producer. No cheap sweetness here. Dry with citrus and pears notes. Don't mix. Enjoy on it's own.
Great top of the range prosecco. Crisp and dry, persistent bubbles, not too acidic, lovely apple and pear flavours, a pleasure to drink.
Drinking a good Prosecco. Nose filled with citru, lemon, orange. Acidic, dry with nice bubbles. Grapefruit, orange, and lemon on the nose.
Beautiful nose of green apple, pear and grapefruit zest. On the palate is has beautiful dry acidity with loads of grapefruit and citrus.
Very nice complement from a hotel in Cap Cana, Dominican Republic! Notes of lemon, good little bubbles. Let the very short holiday begin!
Afternoon post-work Prosecco - Dry, crisp and fresh, more champagne like than the other stuff we’d tasted. Honey, green apple, bit of lime.
A very nice, summertime sipper. Good acidity and just the slightest touch of sweetness to make it extremely drinkable. Enjoy with friends.
Pleasant and smooth double. Balanced extra dry simple but tasty. I would say pear is the main flavour. Perfect with Japanese food.
pear peach apple lemon minerals A very nice Prosecco well made, with great acidity, structure, intense bubbling and nice finish.
Semi fruity and floral smells. Very bubbly dry almost champagne  apple citrus pear. Light and refreshing. Crispy and finishes smooth
👃: apple, straw, lemon, honey, melon, cream, minerals and grass 👅: green apple, citrus, pear, lime, strawberry, brioche and cheese
A pleasant surprise to find in an airport lounge! Nice fizz, lots of peach, citrus and almonds. There is a hint of toast too.
Very nice Prosecco. Medium sweetness and dryness make it very pleasant to drink, with nice hints of apple and melon. Recommended.
Citrus and tree fruit forward, some minerality and breadiness. Great combination of acid, bubbles, and lower sugar, lovely Prosecco
Very good quality prosecco docg. Pear lime lemon white peach toast bread dough pastry. M+ acid m alcohol m body fine creamy persistent bubbles
For the price point, I thought this was one of the better proseccos I've had. It had notes mushrooms and was not very sweet. Well balanced.
Light yellow color. Nose of citrus. Light body, plenty of effervescence, dry. Notes of lemon, orange, pear and minerals. A delicious wine!
This paired nicely with a cruise along the Amalfi coast! Very refreshing and a good amount of bubbles, this is a must try from Santa Margherita.
This is a fine inexpensive Prosecco. Great crisp bubbles, light taste and texture, well balanced. Thanks, Santa Margherita, you’re a gem.
Light and bubbly. Lemon, pear, and nice minerality. The rest of the bottle shall be mimosas! With my bride @[1|35855746|Kayla DiFazio]
Light pear bouquet. Medium effervescence. Easy drinking. Not much tannin. Apple, peach, and nectarine flavours. Medium finish.
Hello gorgeous. Gorgeous and refreshing. Light and bubbly. So smooth and luxurious. This is how I like my fizzy wines. Prosecco at its finest.
Quite fraigrant -  top notes apple, citrus and peach. A good 4/5. Good on a hot day.  had it for new year again - super Lovely brut.
Had this with family Christmas celebration! They really love this! Will need to get this again or other Valdobbiadene Prosecco
Clear, bubbly, with aromas of citrus, fruits, very smooth, balanced. Great with cheese, desserts, pastry. Fantastic Prosecco!
It's our final night in mandatory hotel quarantine and these bubbles taste like freedom is coming. #setusfree  #quarantinewines2021
Fiji 2025 🏝️ trip. Dinner at Daikoku Japanese 🇯🇵 restaurant with @[1|10298079|Matteus Ricci] and this nice Prosecco to start off the night.
Really solid prosecco. Fine bubbles, pale yellowish starw hue. Yeast and lemon on the nose, same on the palate. Not overly dry, refreshing acidity.
Always reliable, nothing special but delicious nonetheless!   Lemon, biscuit, hint of peach, fine racy bubbles. Crisp and fresh!
Light, refreshing, and light bubbles. Great notes of apple and pear. I would recommend this to anyone looking to celebrate a promotion or special occasion. Excellent.
DOCG prosecco from valdobbiadene. Fresh and zesty on the nose. Pear, crushed apple, citrus. Brut. High acidity, short and balanced finish. A good quality prosecco
Not much fizz, but a smooth, lovely drop overall.   Sight: very young pale yellow  Fresh clean nose with a hint of Lemon Hint of lemon and peach on the palette.
In the words of "makin mattresses" bellisimo!  Crisp green apple, gorgeous  mouthfeel and lovely finish. Dry with the perfect amount of sweetness. Nailed it!
NV tasted Sep 2020. As with the previous tasting it showed crispness, considerable apple and buttery characters and went down all too easily! Great value, too.
Probably the best tasting Prosecco I’ve had in a while. Crisp and dry with fine bubbles. Light fruit bouquet of apples , subtle citrus and minerality.
One of the best Prosecco I have ever tried. Price quality rate is also very successful. Layered, clean citrus aromas with slight touch of sugar. Lovely!
Vivid light golden colour. Lots of stone fruit and apple on the nose with a shortbread hint. Lighter bodied, dry and small continuous bubbles. Higher acidity.
In Orlando FL USA Elegant and refined DOCG Prosecco Superiore. Fresh fruit notes of pear, apple citrus.plenty of bubbles. Crisp finishing. Recommended!
Blooming sourdough bread aroma followed closely by hints of lemon. I definitely sensed a grass note on the palate but the flavour of green apple is much stronger
Crisp pear and citrus aromas and flavors with a touch of yeast.  Nice Prosecco but Santa Margherita wines typically come at a price point above their quality.
apple pear peach lemon minerals lemon lime stone grapefruit stone fruit almond brioche brioche vanilla orange cheese slate yellow apple yellow apple green pear
Very nice and typical Prosecco apple pear citrus peach green apple lemon minerals honey lime melon grapefruit stone straw cream yeast toast apricot brioche almond
Another nice experience today. Santa Margarita does not dissapoint you. 😄 Mainly apple, pear, citrus, peach, lemon, green apple, minerals, stone and some yeast. 👍
A fresh acidic backbone with fine, persistent bubble lends verve. Fresh apple, lemon and honey abound. The finish teases further white peach and minerals.
For the price, this offer a lot of value. We used it at 1st to make spritz but we quickly changed our mind and drink it by itself!  Maybe I’m not a cocktail person 🤷🏼‍♂️
Lufthansa Lounge Cologne. Really nice. Two above avg Prosecco options on offer in the lounge. Clean, fresh and zippy. Green Apple with a nice light finish.
A really good, close to perfect combination of pears, apples, peach and floral notes made this a perfect choice right before tonights mediyoga-lesson. 🙏 Namaste 🙏
Great prosecco. very refreshing, hint of elderberry and flower tones. Slight sweetness without it being sugary sweet. Reminds me of a Blanc de.blancs wine.
Aromas of green apple, citrus, vanilla. Palate is surprisingly creamy, adds honey, grapefruit & lime. Bubbles are light, but quite fine for a Prosecco.
Just as a prosecco should be-light, bubbly, unassuming. No notes out of place. Very drinkable and perfect to share with those unfamiliar with prosecco.
Pale lemon color with M NI, there is blossom, lemon, lime and apple in the note. It's crisp, M+ acidity, M alcohol and body. bubble is delicate, M FI and finish. good bottle
Apparently this happens to be sparkling wine to celebrate 🍾  new year. Without knowing we bought this wine for new year's eve already 2 times and both times we enjoyed it.
3.8/5.0 Nice prosecco, from autentic area and superior level. fresh bubbles, delicate aromas of white flowers, peach, green apple. Medium acidity, well mousse.  Resume: from good to very good
First time to try this prosecco and I really enjoyed the bolder flavor.  It was enjoyed on Christmas Day as Mimosa.  Usually OJ overpowers the prosecco but this one was great
Pale yellow color.  Green apple, citrus,  peach on the nose. Bubbles not fine and lasting, but palate enjoys a crispy and nice acid touch. Little minerals on finish. Great for family meeting!
a really nice brut prosecco, quite savory with certain yeast and bready characteristics. Still a nice lemon zing with a very mineral finish to it. I really appreciate this wine !!
Pale in color. In nose, peach, green apple, lemon peel and floral aromas. In taste, medium sweetness, medium to full acidity, mineral, peach and green apple notes un palate with very soft bubbles.
i am not a sparkling wine lover but this is very nice. dry and it has an aroma similar to ehrenfelser and gewurztraminer combination. tree fruit flavor, sparkling is perfect
Appearance: pale lemon  Nose: medium intensity, lemon, green apple, minerals  Palate: dry, intense carbonation, medium alcohol, light body, medium intensity, medium finish  Quality: very good
Very aromatic, fruity, slight touch of smoke (perhaps oak), peach, apricot, pear and almonds on the nose. Touch of bitterness on the palate and noticeably residual sugar.
Amazingly smooth and remember able prosecco. It was fruity, refreshing, and very drinkable yet still with character. A touch too sweet for my taste but nonetheless remarkable.
I’m not usually a Prosecco gal but this one is really interesting! Super crisp and really bubbly.  citrus, pear, green apple and dry. Bubbles linger. Great for a special occasion
Fantastic value! Apples and pears on the nose leading to flavors of stone fruit,melon and orange. Fresh and lively with a tight bead that continues leaving a clean,dry, slightly yeasty finish.
Definitely a notch above the average prosecco. Beautiful gold emerald tint . Honey, apricot , and nectarine comes through very lightly , excellent body . Classical and beautiful expression.

Refreshing Prosecco Valdobbiadene. Pale yellow in color with fine bubbles.Lovely notes of peach, lemon and sweet bread. Dry, fresh acidity, with fruit and minerals on the palate,
Light prosecco with intense bubbles classic flavours apple pear citrus. Couldn’t believe to find it in Dan Murphy’s for $18 what a treat
Retasting, same notes. Light straw colour. Nose of apple, citrus, pear, minerals and honey. Fizzy, very fruity and fresh. Good balance of sweetness and acidity, with a dry finish. Give it 3.9
Pale yellow in the glass with fine abundant and persistent bubbles. Notes of yeast and apple on the nose. pear, apple and citrus on the palate.  Should be perfect for our Bellins 😋
Green apples and green melon on the nose, lively and bubbly, green apples on the tongue. Noticeably acidic yet it is restrained by a gentle sweetness. To be purchased again.
One of the best prosecco - valdobbiadenes always are - crisp and quite yeasty for being a Prosecco. Less sweet than most, deeper and complex, citrus notes and great structure/perlage
Light gold color, lovely bubbles  Nose: cream, citrus, campfire, slight chamomile   Palette: smoked meats, Apple, honeysuckle,
Green apple, melon. A really lovely Prosecco! Paired well with our snacks: truffle popcorn, smoked salmon, and soft cheeses. A DOCG Superiore.   Tried in a virtual tasting with Co-Op.
Some mainstream prosecco superiore tasting real nice. Lemon, red apple, pear and some floral honey quality. It is light, crisp and very nice texturally. Great value for some good juice!!
Really enjoyed how fruity this was without being excessively acidic or sweet at all. I picked up tropical fruits like pineapple and melon with a little bit of orange rind. Very enjoyable.
Pale lemon in color. Medium intensity aroma of green apple, nectarine, pear, lemon, honeysuckle, wet stone. Dry, medium plus acidity, medium alcohol, light body and medium finish. Very elegant.
This Prosecco was given to me as a gift and I really enjoyed it. It is not sweet, which I prefer. I will definitely purchase this again. In fact, I believe I may have had it before.

Good Prosecco with a light yellow color in the aroma and taste, mainly apple pear melon peach in the background citrus fruit notes of earthy ageing vegetal floral.  Prosecco is well-balanced with high acidity. A crisp mineral with a well-structured bubble. Good wine as an aperitif.  🥂👍🏻😃👌🏻

Bubbly warm up before the start of the New Year’s party at my house.... Everything is ready ... just waiting for my friends to arrive..😝  Voluminous effervescence Aromas of golden delicious  apple, Bartlett pear, white peach, hint of slivered almond, honey and white flowers Flavors of apples, lemon curd & hints of pineapples Light bodied, dry, well balanced  Very refreshing finish  Gives me the right buzzz before  The New Year night Extravaganza !!🎊  Tada!!🥂🍾💃🏻🔥

Light and easy drinking sparkling wine with soft creamy mousse. Valdobiaddene is DOCG procecco producing area. Aromas of floral, white pepper (due to sparkles), pear, nectarines and fruits from warm area. The palate is soft medium acidity with bitterness in the end.

Light fizziness, elegant, floral and fruity.  Aroma👃🏼 baked apple, pear, apricot, lavender. Palate 👄 dried apricot, plum, sweet orange, white peach, fig, brioche. Great with veggie salads, fresh seafood and light bites.  Cheers 🥂 🌟

Interesting style. Nose is full of minerality, vanilla, oak,  ginger, apple, lime peel, goat cheese. Very structured.   Med bodied on the palate with well balanced dryness and slight sweetness. Fine persistent mousse. Plenty of elegant, clear notes of fresh pear, green apple driven medium acidity before straw, hay, grass, gooseberry, strawberry, raspberry become apparent. Finished with cheese, almond, brioche.  Aftertaste of wet stone, minerality, chalk, yeast.  Very versatile, Complement ice cream,  pastry.

I’ve rated this a couple times but I figured I will see how my palate changes. This Prosecco is a classic for me on lazy Sundays! Some peach and apple sweetness on the nose. Nice and easy on the palate with good fizz, apple, pear, citrus, and some honey. Great for a spritz or by itself. 🥂

3,8 ⭐️ This Santa Margherita Valdobbiadene Prosecco is a nice drink for all kinds of celebrations.

DATA_COMMENTO: The winery is situated a bit north west of Verona.
The Glera grape was originally named Prosecco. Her name was changed 2010 by a law of the Italian ministry of agriculture. Prosecco is now only a region of wine origin.

TESTO_ORIGINALE: Excellent price performance Prosecco!  Glera from Valdobbiadene and Conegliano hills. Charmat method.  Pale straw color with fine delicate bubbles.  Pear, apple, ripe pineapple and sweet lemon notes.  Medium acidity, nice fizziness, high astringency and long finish with peach, ripe pear and pineapple aftertaste.  3.9 - 89/100  Wine.com 91, WE 89

Color is pale, lemon. Nose is medium plus intensity. Aromas of pear, lemon, honeysuckle, green apple, lime, white peach, pear drop, green grapes  Palate is off-dry, high acidity, med alcohol, medium body, medium flavour intensity. Flavours of pear, lemon, honeysuckle green apple, lime, white peach pear drop, green grapes. Medium finish. Creamy mousse. Quality Very good. Not suitable for ageing.

A really good one. Again from Santa margerita on the lighter side of brut. Low bubbles (could be open for a while @lh lounge) but the taste awards it. Light pear, Apple, citrus with a sweety finish. Great Pro.
I enjoyed this very much. If it was the fresh taste of apples or if it was the jacuzzi ambience is hard to say. Can reccomend both.
Very good and concentrated Prosecco.  apple and pear present. Very ripe.  Followed by citrus and yeast. Fairly long, soft finish.
Pale gold, medium–large bubbles. White flowers, smoky aromas. Limes, tangerines, red apples. Sweetness close to extra dry. Good choice for weekend breakfast.
Fresh Prosecco with longest aftertaste so far. Many citrus on palate: green apple, lime, grapefruit, so it's extra dry with more sugar than brut is nicely balanced.
Classic medium prosecco with medium body and fizziness. green apple citrus and tropical hints with minerals. Nice one with really good price value ratio
Pale straw colour, medium sized and intense bubbles. Green apple, pear, vanilla, a lot of citrus (lemon and lime). Not a wonderful one, but a bit more than intermediate one 🥂

Although Prosecco isn’t as complex as a classic method sparkling, this Valdobbiadene example is making an impression. Very crisp, with distinct and fresh fruit profile. Green apple, green pear, lemon zest aromas. Lovely mousse, high but pleasant acidity, and great flavor intensity on palate. Medium finish. Much better than your average Prosecco.

Fresh bouquet of apple, lemon and grapefruit.  Creamy texture in the mouth with refreshing acidity, a long-lasting oakiness and unusual saffron aftertaste. Great value and really enjoyed this with a seafood risotto. Rated close to 4 for the price.

Bought a huge bag of fresh mushrooms from the flea market and the dinner was accordingly designed!  A very light and enjoyable prosecco, that preserves the correct dry touch, sensational small bubbles and the alluring aromas and taste of green apples with brioche.  Paired well with mushrooms in cream sauce and wine accompanied by a classic potatoe salad.  Enjoy!!

pale, golden straw color and sparkles with lively effervescence. Opening with aromas of fresh-picked citrus and honeysuckle blossoms, the crisp, clean palate brings fruity flavors of green apple, juicy peach and ripe lemon, framed by hints of minerality. The finish is light and refreshing with a tantalizing hint of sweetness.

Nice prosecco with a pale green/gold colour & really fine bead. Pear, apple, ripe but crisp with a hint of almond in the finish. Only problem is it disappeared waaaay to quickly. Merry Christmas 2018 to all 🌲🍾🥂
Enjoyable and pleasant to drink bubbles....easy drinking, fruity and dry. Well done prosecco....cheers! 🍾🥂👍
Fresh, crisp, dry. Pale clear yellow colour. Clean taste of pear & apple. Not strong on acidity, but still interesting.
Maintain that this is bloody good Prosecco. Pear drop, lemon sherbet and minerality, plus honey and grapefruit.
Citrus flavours, grapefruit, lemon lime and ever so slight bread notes. Very enjoyable and refreshing
Creamy, apple and citrus combined to create a great Italian sparkling wine. Very good indeed.
Green apples. Lingering taste. Refreshing.
Really well done prosecco! Cheers 🥂
Flavourful and complex, with a lot of character, high acidity, apple citrus cream notes. Unfortunately bubbles don’t last too long.
